report_name: Visa Launches Visa Threat Intelligence Platform (VTIP) to Proactively Fight Cyber and Payments Fraud

threat_actors.name: Financial Fraud Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: A general classification for threat actors who breach merchant or financial institution systems using malware, phishing, or vulnerability exploits to steal payment card data and commit fraud. This is a conceptual actor representing the threats VTIP is designed to combat.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: Used by threat actors to steal credentials for payment gateways, processing platforms, or banking portals as a precursor to payment fraud, a threat category monitored by VTIP.

attack_patterns.name: Credential API Hooking
attack_patterns.description: A technique associated with financial malware on point-of-sale (POS) systems to steal card data, as described in the VTIP threat overview.

attack_patterns.name: Forge Web Credentials
attack_patterns.description: A method of credential theft and abuse against payment platforms that the Visa Threat Intelligence Platform provides intelligence on.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: A primary threat vector used to breach merchants and financial institutions to initiate payment fraud schemes, which VTIP helps to identify.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: Used in phishing campaigns that spoof a financial institution's brand to trick customers, a threat category monitored by VTIP.

malware.name: Financial Malware
malware.description: A general category of malware, including Point-of-Sale (POS) malware, designed to steal card data from POS systems or e-commerce websites. VTIP is designed to provide IOCs for such malware.

identities.name: Visa
identities.description: Global payments technology company that launched the Visa Threat Intelligence Platform (VTIP) on July 2, 2026.

identities.name: Visa Threat Intelligence Platform (VTIP)
identities.description: A commercial threat intelligence platform launched by Visa to provide financial institutions and merchants with proactive threat intelligence on cyber and payments fraud.

identities.name: VisaNet
identities.description: Visa's global processing network, used as a data source for VTIP to identify fraud patterns and anomalies.

identities.name: Financial Institutions
identities.description: A primary client category for the Visa Threat Intelligence Platform (VTIP).

identities.name: Merchants
identities.description: A primary client category for the Visa Threat Intelligence Platform (VTIP).

identities.name: Payment Processors
identities.description: A primary client category for the Visa Threat Intelligence Platform (VTIP).

infrastructure.name: Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used for phishing campaigns that spoof a financial institution's brand to trick customers, a threat monitored by VTIP.

infrastructure.name: Dark Web Marketplaces
infrastructure.description: Underground marketplaces used for the sale of stolen payment card data, which is monitored by VTIP.

indicators.name: Web Skimmer Script 'skimmer.js' Detection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Web Skimmer Script 'skimmer.js' Access
id: 9c5b1a3d-8f6e-4b1a-9c8d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to a web script named 'skimmer.js', which is a common name for malicious JavaScript code used to steal payment card information from e-commerce websites.
references:
  - https://ffnews.com/news/visa-launches-threat-intelligence-platform-to-shield-financial-institutions-from-cyber-fraud
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '/skimmer.js'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Suspicious Point-of-Sale Process 'POS.exe' Detection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Suspicious Point-of-Sale Process 'POS.exe' Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a process named 'POS.exe', which could be indicative of Point-of-Sale malware.
references:
  - https://ffnews.com/news/visa-launches-threat-intelligence-platform-to-shield-financial-institutions-from-cyber-fraud
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-03
tags:
  - attack.t1003
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\POS.exe'
  condition: selection
